colors.name: night-deep
colors.role: background base — the deep indigo of a city fully asleep, before any streetlight

colors.name: night-mid
colors.role: secondary surface — panels, cards, the slight lift above the page ground

colors.name: moonlight
colors.role: primary text — soft off-white, the colour of a window with one lamp on (16.1:1 on night-deep, AAA)

colors.name: moonlight-dim
colors.role: secondary text — Korean glosses, captions, the rider's inner voice; moonlight composited over night at ~62% (7.8:1 on night-deep, AA+)

colors.name: neon-blue
colors.role: the cold accent — neon signs, streetlamp halos, focus ring, selection, the rider's headlight glow, active status. 7.5:1 on night-deep (AAA on normal text and the page's signature glow)

colors.name: neon-blue-bright
colors.role: neon at full draw — the lit stroke of an SVG neon sign that has just been switched on (10.1:1; large type / non-text only)

colors.name: ember-amber
colors.role: the single warm note — reserved for the meal, the handoff, dawn. Appears once per viewport at most, never on cold UI chrome. 8.3:1 on night-deep; 8.1:1 on the handoff ground

colors.name: steel
colors.role: metadata — chapter numerals, eyebrows, inactive delivery steps, the timestamp of a city that has not woken up (5.7:1 on night-deep, AA on normal text)

colors.name: hairline
colors.role: decorative rules — moonlight at ~12% over night-deep; never carries text

fonts.role: Korean display — the wordmark, the chapter cards. Webtoon-title weight, used sparingly for splash moments
the prose voice — every Korean line of poetry, status, caption. Modern Korean sans that holds its weight in the dark
English display — the MOONLIT wordmark in Latin, chapter numerals, section heads. Quiet grotesque
the machine — delivery tracker timestamps, order numbers, every line that belongs to the app rather than the story

texture: A three-layer night. Base: a slow vertical gradient night-deep → night-mid that holds the whole page like the sky over an empty city. Mid: SVG feTurbulence grain (baseFrequency 0.9, desaturated, ~5% opacity) stepped between fixed offsets under .moonlit-js — the pixel noise of a screen in a dark room, never drift. Top: per-panel radial vignettes and one warm glow gradient reserved for the handoff panel, where a single ember-amber radial bloom leaks in from the right edge as if dawn is breaking through the door.

motion.name: ease-night
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) — reveals and neon draws: settle like the eye adjusting to the dark

motion.name: neon-flicker
motion.value: 3.7s steps(1, end) infinite across three brightness stops — the buzz of a tired neon tube that has been on since dinner

motion.name: neon-draw
motion.value: stroke-dashoffset 1400ms ease-night per sign, staggered 180ms; brightness ramps with the draw

motion.name: status-type
motion.value: 55ms per glyph with a 540ms blink cursor; each delivery status types itself in like a tracker update

motion.name: headlight-trail
motion.value: linear-gradient light streaks translated X at 5.4s linear infinite, opacity scrubbed by IO; speed lines for a moving rider

motion.name: moon-drift
motion.value: linear-gradient 'sky' translate3d on scroll-Y via IO progress; the moon climbs as the page descends

motion.name: chapter-rise
motion.value: 700ms ease-night, translateY(28px) → 0, 90ms stagger — panels rise into the frame like webtoon panels

motion.name: reveal
motion.value: 600ms ease-night, translateY(18px) → 0, 70ms stagger per observer batch

motion.name: ember-bloom
motion.value: 1800ms ease-out radial-gradient opacity 0 → 1 on the handoff panel; the warm note arrives slowly, the way dawn does
